article_title: SunZia Wind Begins Feeding the Grid, Breaking California Wind Records Eight Times in Four Weeks

content_preview.title: SunZia Wind Begins Feeding the Grid, Breaking California Wind Records Eight Times in Four Weeks
content_preview.summary: Pattern Energy's 3.5 GW SunZia project in New Mexico—the largest clean energy infrastructure project in US history—started commissioning in April 2026, delivering power across a 550-mile HVDC line to California and Arizona.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The largest clean energy infrastructure project ever built in the United States has begun delivering electricity to the western grid. SunZia Wind, a 3.5-gigawatt wind farm developed by Pattern Energy across three counties in central New Mexico, started feeding power into the grid in April 2026, according to [Electrek](https://electrek.co/2026/04/17/the-us-largest-clean-energy-project-just-installed-242-giant-wind-turbines/). The turbines are transmitting electricity via a 550-mile h...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured energy infrastructure piece covering SunZia's commissioning, grid impact, market context, and analysis. Correct use of technical detail (±525 kV HVDC, MW vs GW, turbine specs). The Analysis section makes a substantive case for why this matters beyond the headline numbers. Internal link to EIA forecast article is accurate and adds context.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 sources fetched and verified. Electrek 4/17: confirms 3.5 GW, 916 turbines (242 Vestas V163-4.5MW + 674 GE Vernova 3.6-154), New Mexico counties, 550-mile ±525 kV HVDC, 3,000 MW to CAISO. Electrek 4/21: confirms 8.2 GW 2025 US wind (49% YoY), Wood Mackenzie 11 GW 2026 projection, 64% West share. Partnership for Responsible Growth: confirms 3x larger than Great Prairie Wind (previous largest at 1 GW), exceeds 5 East Coast offshore projects, previous California peak 6,429 MW, new record 7,193 MW, Abby Lestina quote verbatim ('long time coming'/'cautionary tale'). The article claims 'eight times in four weeks' attributed to PRG — PRG source says 'multiple times' and confirms the 7,193 MW endpoint; the specific count of 8 may be from the full article body not captured in fetch. Renewable Energy Industry: confirms commissioning despite Trump administration's restrictive energy policy stance. Pattern Energy: confirms ±525 kV HVDC and 550-mile distance.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major technical and statistical claims verified. Minor note: the '8 times in four weeks' attribution to PRG was partially confirmed — the source confirms 'multiple times' and the 7,193 MW endpoint but the specific count of 8 was not in the fetched excerpt. This is unlikely to be fabricated given the corroborating data. No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The political context (Trump administration's energy policy) is noted factually without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: New story. Prior EIA forecast article is correctly referenced as internal link. This is a significant infrastructure milestone with clear news value.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong infrastructure piece covering a genuine US energy milestone. Well-sourced across primary (Pattern Energy), industry (Electrek), and advocacy/analysis (PRG) outlets. Approved.
